reasoning: The question asks if EU gas imports from Russia will reach at least 15 bcm in any quarter of 2025, according to the Bruegel dataset. In 2024, total Russian gas imports to the EU were 52 bcm (an average of 13 bcm per quarter). The highest quarter in 2024 (Q4) reached around 13.4 bcm. In January 2025, the pipeline transit via Ukraine ended, completely severing a route that delivered approximately 14-16 bcm/year (about 3.5 to 4 bcm per quarter). This leaves only TurkStream (capacity ~15.75 bcm/year to Europe) and LNG (imported ~20 bcm in 2024). Even if TurkStream and LNG both operate at maximum historical capacity, the total would be only ~36 bcm/year, or roughly 9 bcm per quarter. Reaching 15 bcm in any single quarter of 2025 is physically impossible without a massive and unprecedented increase in LNG terminal throughput dedicated exclusively to Russian gas or a reopening of the Ukraine transit route, both of which conflict with current EU policy (REPowerEU phase-out goals and active efforts to diversify). Thus, I assign the lowest allowable probability (2%).
